highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.108 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a new `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` environment variable to opt into a 1-hour prompt cache TTL, which can significantly reduce API costs for long sessions. It also introduces a recap feature to help maintain context across conversations. If you're using Bedrock, note that `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H_BEDROCK` is now deprecated in favor of the unified variable.

highlights.title: Claude Code v2.1.105 Released
highlights.summary: This release adds a `path` parameter to the `EnterWorktree` tool, making it easier to switch between Git worktrees inside your repo — useful if you manage multiple feature branches simultaneously. It also introduces `PreCompact` hook support, letting you block context compaction by returning a specific exit code or JSON decision, giving you more control over Claude's memory management. Background monitor support for plugins is also included.

highlights.title: Claude Code OAuth Down for Over 12 Hours
highlights.summary: Users on Claude Code v2.1.107 reported being unable to complete OAuth authentication, with the prompt stuck on the 'Paste code here if prompted' step and no way to paste the code. This issue appeared under-reported outside of Reddit and Discord, prompting the HN thread to call for higher urgency from Anthropic. If you hit this issue, consider rolling back to an earlier version or checking the official Discord for workarounds.

highlights.title: The Vercel Plugin on Claude Code Wants to Read Your Prompts
highlights.summary: A developer discovered that the Vercel plugin for Claude Code includes telemetry that can access your prompts, raising privacy concerns for users who rely on third-party plugins. This is a reminder to audit any Claude Code plugins you install, especially those from third parties, before granting them access to your workspace. Review plugin permissions carefully and check for telemetry opt-outs in plugin settings.

highlights.title: Depwire – Codebase Dependency Graph and MCP Server for AI Coding Assistants
highlights.summary: Depwire is an open-source tool that generates a dependency graph for your codebase and exposes it as an MCP server, allowing Claude Code to better understand how files and modules relate to each other. This can improve the quality of AI-generated refactors and impact analysis by giving Claude structured context about your project's architecture. It's a great complement to Claude Code for medium-to-large codebases.

highlights.title: Mastering Claude Code in 30 Minutes
highlights.summary: This YouTube tutorial offers a concise, time-boxed walkthrough of Claude Code's core features, making it ideal for developers who want a quick but thorough introduction without a multi-hour course. It covers key workflows and practical usage patterns you can apply immediately in your projects. A great starting point if you're new to Claude Code and want to get productive fast.

highlights.title: ByteDance Expert's Comprehensive Claude Code Full-Stack Tutorial: From Beginner to Pro (Bilibili)
highlights.summary: This Bilibili course is described as the most systematic Claude Code and automation workflow tutorial on the platform, deeply optimized for Claude 3 series models and targeting both newcomers and experienced developers. It walks through real development scenarios end-to-end, helping you build a complete mental model of AI-assisted development with Claude Code. If you're a Chinese-speaking developer looking for structured learning, this series is a strong resource.

tips_of_the_day: Before installing any Claude Code plugin from a third party, check its source code or README for telemetry disclosures — some plugins may collect prompt data by default, and you should opt out or avoid them if privacy is a concern.

Set the `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` environment variable in your shell profile to reduce API costs on long Claude Code sessions ��� a 1-hour cache TTL means repeated context won't be re-billed on every request.

If you're managing multiple feature branches, try using the `EnterWorktree` tool with the new `path` parameter to let Claude Code operate directly inside a specific Git worktree, keeping your main workspace clean while AI edits happen in isolation.
